message:
fix(arithmetic): use i128 intermediate for millionths rate calculations to prevent overflow

Four rate/ratio calculations that multiplied a counter by MILLION (1,000,000)
before dividing would silently overflow i64 for large inputs. Upgrade each
to use i128 for the intermediate product:

- PolicyComparisonReport::divergence_rate_millionths
- FailoverController::failover_success_rate_millionths
- QueueArtifact::completion_rate_millionths
- Novelty entropy bonus in synthesize_candidate (also remove redundant
  .min(200_000) clamp since % 200_001 modulus already bounds the result)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
